title: orthocarpus cuspidatus subsp. copelandii
intro: Orthocarpus cuspidatus subsp. copelandii, also known as Copeland's bird's beak, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the southwestern United States. It is a type of flowering plant belonging to the figwort family, and its name is derived from the shape of its flowers, which resemble a bird's beak. This plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The Copeland's bird's beak plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function.
